title: Pilea umbellata
taxonomy: Pilea umbellata is classified under the family Urticaceae. This family includes various plants with stinging hairs, to which Pilea umbellata belongs. Pilea umbellata is a species of flowering plant within the genus Pilea. The genus Pilea is a group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Pilea umbellata is closely related to other Pilea species in terms of its characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Pilea umbellata is known to have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. Its leaves are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. The plant's medicinal properties are often attributed to its unique combination of bioactive compounds.

intro: The Pilea umbellata, also known as the umbrella pilea, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and a member of the Urticaceae family. Traditionally, Pilea umbellata has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Pilea umbellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and free radical-scavenging activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for its purported medicinal benefits.
